# unch v0.3.0

`v0.3.0` turns `unch` into a more stable multi-model local search tool. This release adds named embedding model selection, protects active indexes from partial rebuilds by isolating snapshots per model, introduces a real benchmark runner, and makes the CLI and CI surface much easier to use day to day.

## Highlights

- adds first-class model selection with `--model embeddinggemma`, `--model qwen3`, or a direct `.gguf` path
- auto-downloads known GGUF models by model id instead of requiring a fully spelled path every time
- adds model-specific runtime defaults so `embeddinggemma` and `qwen3` no longer share one hardcoded `ctx-size` and `batch-size`
- introduces snapshot-based, model-scoped index storage so a failed rebuild for one model no longer damages the active index for another model
- adds richer CLI help with command-specific help pages and clearer model guidance
- adds a benchmark runner with pinned suites, quality scoring, JSON output, and progress logs in CI-friendly terminal output
- fixes benchmark indexing semantics so warm index runs can reuse cached local embeddings instead of recomputing the full embedding set on every repeat
- adds `unch remote download --commit <sha> ...` to fetch a published search artifact for one specific commit without binding the checkout to remote sync
- moves default CI workflows to Linux-first runners while keeping benchmark runs on release tags
- adds a multi-platform test matrix for regular CI across Linux and macOS, while coverage publishing stays on Ubuntu
- uses a lightweight smoke profile for release-tag benchmark CI so tagged builds stay reasonably fast while still publishing benchmark artifacts
- orders the tagged benchmark job ahead of coverage in CI so the benchmark summary is surfaced first on release runs

## Breaking changes

- the local `index.db` schema is now model-scoped and snapshot-based
- existing local indexes should be rebuilt with `unch index` after upgrading
- searching with a model now requires an active index for that same model family; for example, `unch search --model qwen3 ...` expects a completed `unch index --model qwen3`

## Upgrade notes

- if you already have a local index from `v0.2.x`, rebuild it after upgrading:
  `unch index`
- if you switch models, rebuild with that model before searching:
  `unch index --model qwen3`
- `--ctx-size` and `--batch-size` now default to the selected model profile when left at `0`; `qwen3` uses larger defaults than `embeddinggemma`
- remote users should rerun the `searcher` workflow after upgrading so the published artifact uses the new snapshot-based schema
- benchmark CI now runs on release tags, while the regular coverage/test workflow still runs on normal pushes
- the release-tag benchmark workflow now uses `1 cold index + 1 warm index + 1 warm search repeat` per smoke-suite case instead of the heavier default local benchmark settings
- benchmark `cold` now means a fresh local index rebuild with embeddings recomputed, while `warm` keeps the existing local index and should reuse cached embedding rows for unchanged symbols
